key_insight: A two-level Schwarz coarse solver with a structured, non-nested global coarse space can replace AMG as the last-level solver in p-multigrid for Poisson problems.
engineering_relevance: This offers a scalable path for pressure Poisson solves in incompressible Navier–Stokes simulations on exascale systems, reducing reliance on black-box AMG.
method: Local solve in the original p-multigrid coarse space
Global coarse solve on a novel structured, non-nested coarse space
Communication-free interpolation between local and global levels
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparative experiments against BoomerAMG using Nek5000/RS on Summit and Frontier, indicating evaluation on highly scalable HPC workloads.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the structured coarse space and the cost of integrating it into existing p-multigrid/solver pipelines.
paper_title: CoarseSolvers for Exascale Solution of Poisson Problems
